title: Functions, Multiple Results, Closures, Recursion, and Contracts · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A rule engine needs small composable functions, explicit failure results, isolated stateful closures, and bounded recursive traversal. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a composable rule and transformation library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design function signatures, named types, parameters, results, and first-class function values around one responsibility.
Use multiple results and the blank identifier without discarding failure or evidence that the caller needs.
Define and call variadic functions, distinguish the slice parameter, and forward existing slices with explicit ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Function Signatures Values · changed case · Quiz Function Signatures Values
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Build 3 independent rule closures with thresholds 15 and 21; preserve multiple-result failure evidence, forward one variadic slice deliberately, and prove recursion reaches a bounded base case. Competency probe: Design function signatures, named types, parameters, results, and first-class function values around one responsibility.

Which action demonstrates Function Signatures Values without dropping earlier contracts?
